title: <name> and the Spellbook Under the Banyan Tree

pages.text: In the heart of her village, near the pond and the fields of sunflowers, stood a giant old banyan tree. One breezy afternoon, <name> sat beneath it, finishing her drawing. Suddenly — thunk! — something shiny dropped from the branches above.

It was a dusty old book with golden edges and a lock shaped like a leaf. As soon as she touched it, the lock clicked open, and glowing letters floated out, swirling like fireflies. 'What’s this?' <name> whispered. The letters formed words: 'To those who dream and dare, magic waits everywhere.'

Her pet parrot, Mitthu, fluttered down from the branch. 'Looks shiny! Read it, <name>!' She giggled. 'Okay, let’s try a spell!' She cleared her throat and said: 'Choco-loco, mango moon, make my flowers dance in tune!' Whoosh! The flowers in her garden started wiggling and singing 'La-la-la!'

Mitthu squawked. 'My turn! My turn!' He peered at the book and read, 'Laddoo-baddoo, tasty soon!' BOING! Her lunchbox popped open, and the laddoos began bouncing all over the room! 'Catch them!' cried <name>, laughing as one laddoo bounced into Mitthu’s beak. 'Delicious chaos!' said Mitthu with a grin.

But when <name> turned another page, the spellbook glowed red. The words shimmered: 'Before sunset, magic must rest — or forever it will be a guest.' 'Oh no!' said <name>. 'If we don’t fix this, everything will stay enchanted!' Together, they raced around — catching floating pencils and calming the giggling broom.

Finally, <name> chanted, 'Undo, redo, back in line, thank you magic, now be fine!' A soft breeze whooshed through the banyan tree, and the world went still. The flowers stopped singing, the laddoos rolled to a stop, and Mitthu blinked. 'No more bouncing sweets?'

<name> laughed. 'Not today, silly bird!' Just then, the spellbook shimmered, winked its golden cover, and vanished — leaving behind one glowing green feather. <name> tucked it behind her ear. 'Maybe magic isn’t gone,' she whispered. Mitthu winked. 'Or maybe it’s just waiting for the next rhyme!'
